data.title: Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Slips as Xbox Struggles With a Consistency Problem
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) stock was down on Friday following news of trouble at Xbox. Xbox CEO Asha Sharma warned that the gaming division expects its revenue to drop by 3% year-over-year. This drop can be attributed to multiple factors, such as inconsistent video game releases.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

According to Windows Central, Xbox hasn't been able to release consistently successful games to increase its revenue. Instead, a handful of popular titles that have performed well have had to absorb the losses suffered by less successful games. Some of the Xbox games with strong sales include Age of Empires, Flight Simulator, and Forza Horizon. However, other games, such as Avowed, The Outer Worlds 2, South of Midnight, Hellblade 2, and Forza Motorsport have failed to reach sales goals.

In addition to lackluster game releases, Xbox also has a development problem. The company's game studios are spending too much money and taking too long to put out games. This has resulted in heavy investments in games that haven't performed well. Additionally, Xbox has had to close some studios that were burning through cash, including Perfect Dark developer The Initiative. There have also been reports that more layoffs are coming to Xbox soon.

Microsoft Stock Movement Today

Microsoft stock was down 0.57% on Friday, extending a 19.34% fall year-to-date. The shares have also dropped 17.82% over the past 12 months.

MSFT stock trading activity today was muted, as some 21 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the stock's three-month average daily trading volume is about 36 million shares.

Is Microsoft Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for Microsoft is Strong Buy, based on 35 Buy and two Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average MSFT stock price target of $557.64, representing a potential 43.67% upside for the shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: BREAKING: SpaceX Stock (SPCX) Erupts 40% Minutes Into Nasdaq Debut
data.description:
Elon Musk's aerospace and artificial intelligence (AI) firm, SpaceX (SPCX), has officially begun trading on the Nasdaq (NDAQ). The debut marks the largest initial public offering (IPO) in tech history. 

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Shares opened just before noon Eastern time at around $150 and quickly erupted to about $176 in early trading, reflecting strong initial demand. This massive surge reflects staggering gains of over 40% from Thursday's initial IPO price of $135 per share.

SpaceX Stock Surges in Early Nasdaq Trading

Following its Nasdaq debut, SpaceX has seen strong early volatility as trading continues. The company's market capitalization has climbed to about $2.16 trillion, while the stock surged to an intraday high of $168.75 after opening at $150.

On the financial side, SpaceX reported revenue growth of over 15%, reaching approximately $4.69 billion. However, profitability metrics have come under pressure, with net income, diluted earnings per share, and net profit margin all showing declines.

Meanwhile, TipRanks data show that CFRA Research analyst Keith Snyder has initiated coverage of SpaceX stock, assigning it a Sell. He has also set a $115 price target, implying about 32% downside. In contrast, Oppenheimer analyst Timothy Horan has assigned a Buy rating for SPCX, projecting a $190 price target and a 13% upside.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 6/12/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Friday following a bout of early-morning volatility.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The SpaceX (SPCX) initial public offering (IPO) is top of mind for investors. The IPO raised $75 billion at a $1.77 trillion valuation and was priced to open at $135 per share. However, SPCX is expected to open at around $170 on the Nasdaq (NDAQ) exchange due to strong demand. This could lift market sentiment and boost risk appetite among investors.

Consumer Sentiment Uptick Boosted by Lower Inflation Expectations

Elsewhere, the University of Michigan's June preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment staged a strong comeback. The index came in at 48.9, above the estimate of 46 and rising from an all-time low of 44.8 in May.

"This measured improvement in sentiment was widespread, seen across age, education, and political party," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

In addition, expectations of inflation declined notably. The short-term expectation dropped to 4.6% from 4.8%. The long-term expectation tumbled to 3.4% from 3.9%.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Adobe's Loss, Marvell's Win: Top Analyst Bangs the Drum for AI Chip Stock
data.description:
Marvell's (NASDAQ:MRVL) new CFO has yet to make an impact at his new workplace, but he's having quite an impact at his former one today.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

On Thursday, the chipmaker announced that CFO Willem Meintjes will leave his role following 10 years at the company. His position will be taken by Dan Durn, who will start his new job on June 15. Durn most recently served as CFO at Adobe between October 2021 and June 2026. Consequently, ADBE shares were taking a severe beating in Friday's session with Durn's exit further spooking investors already concerned about how Adobe will hold up in the new AI-driven paradigm.

Which doesn't seem to be a concern for Marvell, a company that has emerged as one of the AI trade's big winners, with shares up by 303% over the past year.

The good news, according to B. Riley's Craig Ellis, an analyst who ranks among the top 1% on Wall Street, is that more gains are on the way.

Ellis reiterated a Buy rating on the shares and bumped his price target from $240 to $345, suggesting the stock will deliver returns of 21.5% in the months ahead. (To watch Ellis's track record, click here)

Ellis sees the appointment of Durn as an excellent choice. Before joining Adobe, he served as CFO at multiple semi companies, including GlobalFoundries, Freescale Semiconductor, NXP Semiconductors, and Applied Materials. "Our interaction during his Applied Materials tenure left us favorably impressed with financial execution during a period of geopolitically challenged growth. We believe his capital allocation and strategic and operational-oriented experience fit MRVL's AI build-out well," the 5-star analyst said.  

But that's not the only reason Ellis is bullish here. Following the company's May 27 FQ1 results, CEO Murphy gave his first COMPUTEX keynote, during which Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang made an unscripted appearance and proclaimed that Marvell will be "the next $1 trillion company."

Marvell and Nvidia's partnership includes collaboration on custom silicon through Nvidia IP enablement, wider adoption of NVSwitch and NVLink Fusion, joint development efforts involving Celestial and in-house silicon photonics, as well as 6G and AI-RAN initiatives. After the earnings release, Ellis believed FY28's $16.5 billion revenue target reflected some contribution from NVLink Fusion, although he also thought the revenue opportunity could be substantially larger, a view he continues to hold.

Lastly, on Friday, June 5, it was announced that Marvell would be added to the S&P 500 ahead of trading on June 22, replacing Pool Corp. The stock climbed roughly 10% the following Monday, a move that Ellis thinks was likely driven by passive index-related buying ahead of the inclusion. A place in the market's benchmark index not only "broadens the NT and LT ownership base," but also caps a "remarkable rise" from a $5 billion market cap when Murphy joined around mid-2016 to ~$250 billion, representing a "significant accomplishment" in his tenure.

Turning now to the broader Street view, where most of Ellis's colleagues are also MRVL supporters. With an additional 23 Buys and 4 Holds, the analyst consensus rates the stock a Strong Buy. However, the $252 average price target implies the stock will decline by 11% over the next year. That disconnect likely reflects more on how sharply the stock has rallied than on any weakening in conviction, suggesting that more upward target revisions are likely not be far behind. (See MRVL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: AMD vs. INTC vs. NVDA: GPU Stocks Rise on New Q1 Market Share Data
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Intel (INTC), and Nvidia (NVDA) stocks were up on Friday. These rallies came alongside the latest discrete GPU market share update. Research firm Jon Peddie released GPU shipment data for the first quarter of 2026. Investors will note that GPU shipments held steady for the quarter, which is expected after the holiday season.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

200% short exposure to NVDA with NVDS

What's worth noting is that Nvidia saw a slight dip in its GPU market share, falling 0.4% to 90% from Q4 2025. That still puts it in an incredibly dominant position, leaving AMD and Intel to pick up whatever scraps the chipmaker leaves behind. For AMD, that translated to an 8% market share, which was also down 0.4% from the prior quarter. Intel was the only chipmaker that saw its market share increase. This was boosted to 1%, which represented a 0.4% gain for the CPU and GPU maker.

While AMD may not be gaining on Nvidia's GPU lead in Jon Peddie's data, it did so in the May 2026 Steam Hardware Survey. Investors will also note that AMD's CPU market share increased in that report, helping it close the gap with Intel.

How This News Affected GPU Makers' Stocks

Each of the major GPU makers saw their shares rise on Friday. Breaking that down:

  • AMD stock was up 4.83% as of this writing.
  • Intel stock gained 6.46% this afternoon.
  • Nvidia stock saw a slight 0.1% increase today.

Which of These GPU Makers Do Analysts Favor?

Turning to the TipRanks stock comparison tool, traders can see which of these GPU stocks analysts prefer. AMD and Nvidia both have Strong Buy consensus ratings, while Intel has a Hold rating. However, NVDA stock has the only upside potential at 52.01%. For comparison, AMD has a possible 5.19% downside and Intel's downside potential sits at 27.17%.
data.author: William White

data.title: Exxon Mobil Stock (XOM) Climbs on Talk it is Eyeing-Up Several Takeover Targets
data.description:
Shares in oil giant Exxon Mobil (XOM) gushed higher today on reports that it was lining up a series of takeover targets including Australian peer Woodside.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Early-Stage Discussions

According to a report on Bloomberg, Exxon Mobil has been holding early-stage discussions internally about M&A moves. Woodside is said to be one of several targets the company has been evaluating.

The Bloomberg article said that these deliberations were preliminary and there was no certainty that they would lead to an offer. Exxon and Woodside did not comment for the article.

According to industry experts, an acquisition of Woodside, which is Australia's top gas exporter, would expand XOM's presence internationally and push the company towards LNG where it has trailed rivals such as Shell (SHEL) and TotalEnergies (TTE).

LNG has risen up the Exxon Mobil wish list since the outbreak of the Iran war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. This has put pressure on LNG supply and prompted buyers to look for alternative suppliers outside of the Middle East.

M&A in Full Flow

Woodside has, Bloomberg reported, secured long-term sales agreements with leading buyers across Asia, including in South Korea and Japan, making it an attractive target for companies seeking a foothold in the LNG market.

Exxon's last big deal was the acquisition of U.S. shale producer Pioneer Natural Resources for $60 billion back in 2024.

The company's share price is up over 25% in the year-to-date buoyed by higher oil prices as a result of the conflict in the Middle East. It also has healthy cash flow numbers – see below – which means it has money to spend.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rises as Iran Affirms Deal Progress; SpaceX Makes Debut
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Friday as optimism builds for a U.S.-Iran peace deal.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said a deal has "never been closer." He added that further details will be released soon.

"The Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding has never been closer," said Araghchi in an X post. "Pending its finalization, the media should refrain from entering speculation about its content." President Trump echoed his announcement by sharing Araghchi's statement in a Truth Social post. On Thursday, Trump paused planned strikes against Iran due to progress in negotiations.

Elsewhere, consumer sentiment staged a comeback in June. The University of Michigan's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment rose to 48.9, above the estimate of 46. The index fell to an all-time low of 44.8 in May as gas prices soared higher.

However, the average price of a gallon of gas has dropped to $4.10 from $4.50 over the past month. That helped lift sentiment while also deflating inflation expectations. "Lower-income consumers exhibited a particularly strong sentiment increase, consistent with the fact that gasoline comprises a larger share of their budgets," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

Year-ahead inflation expectations dropped to 4.6% from 4.8%. However, they were at 3.4% before the start of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28. Long-term inflation expectations fell to 3.4% from 3.9%.

Finally, SpaceX (SPCX) made its long-awaited debut on the Nasdaq (NDAQ) exchange. SPCX opened at $150, higher than its IPO price of $135. A total of 555.6 million shares were offered.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.52% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: BlackRock Stock (BLK) Holds Steady Even as Redemption Pressure Jumps in Its Private Credit Funds
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) moved to limit investor withdrawals from two of its private credit funds on June 12, 2026, after redemption requests exceeded their preset quarterly caps. On June 12, 2026, the asset manager activated liquidity gates on two major vehicles – the HPS Corporate Lending Fund (HLEND) and the BlackRock Private Credit Fund (BDEBT) – after investors asked to redeem more than 5% of shares for the quarter. Despite the pressure, BLK stock was up 1.3% on Friday.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The pressure was sharp in BlackRock's HLEND fund, where investors sought to redeem 13.3% of shares, up from 9.3% earlier in 2026. BlackRock said it will repurchase only 5% of the fund, cashing out investors on a pro rata basis.

Further, the BDEBT fund experienced requests to withdraw 5.3% of shares. The fund stuck to its 5% cap, cashing out investors for about $83 million.

Why the Gates Came Down

Private credit funds lend directly to companies through long‑term, illiquid loans. Because these assets cannot be sold quickly without steep discounts, most funds cap withdrawals at 5% per quarter to avoid forced selling. When investors ask for more than that, managers put "gates" in place to protect the fund and keep the portfolio stable.

The jump in withdrawal requests is a clear sign that the once-booming private credit market is under pressure. Investors have grown more careful as concerns rise about loan losses, fraud cases, and exposure to software companies that may be at risk as AI reshapes the industry.

Is BlackRock a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BlackRock stock based on nine Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months. Further, the average BLK price target of $1,234.82 per share implies 20.21% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
